mechanism_narrative: Monthly acceleration in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which raises corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO acceleration fails to produce significantly positive 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals weakening labor income and consumption demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when the UNRATE threshold trigger is met.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous elevation in consumer prices and the federal funds rate creates a profit squeeze through higher input costs and tighter credit, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS does not significantly predict negative 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, stronger-than-trend payroll growth signals persistent wage pressure that forces additional federal funds rate increases, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Above-trend PAYEMS growth during tightening cycles is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month RSAFS is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP signals recession onset, driving negative S&P 500 returns over sixty trading days when occurring late in an expansion.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 contractions during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month acceleration in industrial production and retail sales confirms a virtuous demand-supply cycle that boosts earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Joint acceleration in INDPRO and RSAFS fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate causes an immediate upward repricing of the equity discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.
